deliveryPositioning: brand-aware WeCom Clienteling demo prototype plus solution blueprint

evidence.notes: Evidence ledger completed; assumptions are kept out of UI.

structure.notes: Layout authority, structure DNA, selected direction, and rejected alternatives are recorded.

productLogic.notes: Business axis, role, journeys, page inventory, and native handoff boundaries are defined.

dataModel.notes: Customers, tasks, appointments, metrics, content assets, owners, and states share stable IDs.

pageContracts.notes: A/B/C page depth and decision dimensions are assigned before implementation.

prototype.notes: Prototype starts from the protected WeCom mini-program shell.

qa.notes: Automated QA, rendered QA, self-critique, and anti-generic review are complete.

selfCritique.starterShellDifference: The selected navigation, home narrative, detail-page architecture, and signature interaction are explicitly chosen from the brief rather than inherited from the starter shell.
selfCritique.priorCaseDifference: No prior case is used in this template; when cases exist, compare navigation, home order, module grammar, detail architecture, and signature interaction.
selfCritique.brandClientelingModule: The primary operating module should express the brand's clienteling model through a business axis such as service, appointment, content, product interest, or priority follow-up.
selfCritique.thinPageRisk: C360, task detail, and appointment detail are the highest risk pages because thin information would make the demo feel like a styled shell.
selfCritique.evidenceBackedTerms: Visible terms must be user-confirmed, official-public, or generic-default, with internal-sounding terms replaced by neutral fallbacks.
selfCritique.assumptionsKeptOut: Unconfirmed segments, programs, rooms, campaigns, workflows, system fields, and proprietary role names remain outside the delivered UI.
selfCritique.debrandedDistinctiveness: After removing brand styling, the prototype should still differ through business axis, navigation logic, page architecture, module grammar, and signature interaction.

creativeDivergenceReview.operatingMetaphor: The prototype uses an explicit business operating metaphor such as service concierge, appointment atelier, relationship pulse, content-to-conversation engine, or manager exception review.
transposedMechanisms: Task-inbox grouping becomes a clienteling priority queue with due dates, status, owner, and next action.
Account-dossier structure becomes C360 identity, value, preference, history, and action context.
creativeDivergenceReview.whyThisIsNotJustSkin: The prototype remains different after removing brand styling because its operating axis, navigation logic, home composition, page architectures, and signature interaction are selected from the brief.
creativeDivergenceReview.creativeRiskControl: Creative choices preserve evidence boundaries, WeCom shell constraints, page depth, scan speed, native handoff clarity, and workbench-first behavior.

renderedChecks: native WeCom handoff when in scope
openQuestions: Confirm any internal customer tiers, task types, role names, CRM fields, content assets, and integration boundaries before using them in UI.
